description: A type confusion vulnerability exists in V8's pointer-compression helpers because they used `V8_ASSUME` on tag conditions for values loaded from sandbox memory. If an attacker corrupts a field that is assumed to contain a Smi-tagged compressed value, helpers in `src/common/ptr-compr*.h` and `src/objects/tagged-field-inl.h` can enter undefined behavior during decompression and then dereference an invalid pointer without a real bounds or type check. The root cause is trusting sandbox-controlled tag bits in these low-level pointer and tagged-field decoding helpers.

fixes.description: Patch generated from https://chromium-review.googlesource.com/c/v8/v8/+/6402513 to avoid remote fetch during validation.
